channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Syria latest: Syrians celebrate Bashar Assad's fall as his whereabouts remain unknown
item.description: Crowds gathered in Syria's Damascus on Sunday to celebrate the fall of Bashar Assad’s government with chants, prayers and the occasional gunfire after opposition fighters entered the capital following a stunning advance.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: The spiritual heart of Paris awakens: Notre Dame hosts first Mass since 2019 fire
item.description: PARIS (AP) — Notre Dame Cathedral on Sunday will host its first Mass since the catastrophic fire of 2019, a moment that transcends religious significance to become a powerful symbol of Paris’ resilience.
item.dc:creator: THOMAS ADAMSON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: South Korean prosecutors detain ex-defense chief over martial law imposition
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — South Korean prosecutors on Sunday detained a former defense minister who allegedly recommended last week’s brief but stunning martial law imposition to President Yoon Suk Yeol, making him the first figure detained over the case.
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM and KIM TONG-HYUNG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Syrian government falls to fast-moving rebels, ending 50 years of iron rule by the Assad family
item.description: The Syrian government has collapsed, falling to a lightning rebel offensive that seized control of the capital.
item.dc:creator: Abdulrahman Shaheen And Abby Sewell, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Europe's economy needs help. Political chaos in France and Germany means it may be slower in coming
item.description: Europe's economy has enough difficulties, from tepid growth to trade tensions with the U.S. Dealing with those woes is only getting harder due to the political chaos in the two biggest European countries, France and Germany.
item.dc:creator: David Mchugh And Lorne Cook, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Alone against a renewed insurgency, Assad may face the end of his rule without his strongest allies
item.description: BEIRUT (AP) — The last time Syrian President Bashar Assad was in serious trouble was 10 years ago, at the height of the country’s civil war, when his forces lost control over parts of the largest city, Aleppo, and his opponents were closing in on the capital, Damascus.
item.dc:creator: ZEINA KARAM and SARAH EL DEEB, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Taiwan reports 14 Chinese warships and 4 balloons near the island
item.description: TAIPEI, Taiwan (AP) — China sent 14 warships, seven military aircraft and four balloons near Taiwan between Saturday and Sunday, according to Taiwan’s Defense Ministry, as Beijing ramps up pressure on the island it claims as its own.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Syrian dictator Bashar Assad flees into exile as Islamist rebels conquer country
item.description: A lightening Syrian Islamist offensive over just days contributed to the demise of the over 50-year reign of the Assad Baathist regime in Damascus.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Italian nun among 25 arrested in raid against ‘Ndrangheta mafia
item.description: The nun, 57, is also well-known for her service in Milan's run-down districts. In February she was one of the recipients of the "Golden Panettone", an annual Milanese civic award.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, NewsNation

item.title: Trump back on the world stage at Notre Dame reopening in Paris
item.description: Zelenskyy said his first face-to-face encounter with Trump since the election was “good and productive,” adding they had agreed to continue working together.
item.dc:creator: Tanya Noury, NewsNation

item.title: Assad regime in peril as insurgents near Syria's capital: What to know
item.description: Syrian rebels on Saturday battled government forces in an effort to take control of the crucial city of Homs as they advanced toward the country's capital, Damascus -- moves that catapulted President Bashar al-Assad's nearly 30 years of power into peril, Reuters reported.
item.dc:creator: Juliann Ventura, The Hill

item.title: An explosion destroys an apartment block in a Dutch city, killing at least 3 and injuring others
item.description: THE HAGUE, Netherlands (AP) — An explosion and fire rocked a neighborhood in the Dutch city of The Hague on Saturday, killing three people and injuring other people and destroying several apartments, according to authorities. The cause of the disaster was unclear.
item.dc:creator: MOLLY QUELL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Notre Dame Cathedral reopens its doors for first time since devastating 2019 fire
item.description: France's iconic Notre Dame Cathedral is formally reopening its doors on Saturday for the first time since a devastating fire nearly destroyed the 861-year-old landmark in 2019.
item.dc:creator: Thomas Adamson, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Syrian government falls in stunning end to 50-year rule of Assad family
item.description: The Syrian government fell early Sunday in a stunning end to the 50-year rule of the Assad family after a sudden rebel offensive sprinted across government-held territory and entered the capital in 10 days.
item.dc:creator: Bassem Mroue, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: South Korean president apologizes for declaring martial law ahead of impeachment vote
item.description: South Korean President Yoon Suk Yeol apologized for declaring martial law earlier in the week, as he now prepares for a parliamentary vote on his impeachment.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: South Korean president apologizes after attempting to impose martial law
item.description: South Korea’s President Yoon Suk Yeol apologized on Saturday for declaring martial law in the country for the first time since 1980, which sparked outrage from citizens and lawmakers alike.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: Family of missing journalist Austin Tice says he's alive
item.description: The family of Austin Tice spoke at the National Press Club on Friday, stating that the journalist who was abducted in 2012 while reporting in Syria is alive. “Today is a day full of emotions.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: Middle East latest: Lebanon closes all its land border crossings with Syria except one
item.description: Lebanon is closing all its land border crossings with Syria except for a main one that links Beirut with the Syrian capital Damascus, the General Security Directorate said Friday.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: United Kingdom cracks down on junk food TV advertisements
item.description: The United Kingdom's Department of Health estimates that 7.2 billion calories will be removed from the diets of British children.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Prann, NewsNation

item.title: Romanian court throws out presidential results after Russian interference allegations
item.description: Romania’s top court on Friday wiped out results from the first round of presidential elections and called for a rerun, days after declassified Romanian security documents alleged Russian interference that promoted a far-right outsider candidate.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: Metal detectorist uncovers fragment of Viking sword in the Netherlands
item.description: A 10th-century Viking sword fragment was discovered by a metal detectorist scouring a field in the Netherlands. Further research is being done to find out more about the ancient item.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump heads to France as world leaders seek to curry favor
item.description: President-elect Trump is holding court with foreign leaders as they seek to gain influence and insight into his mindset before he takes office, with the expectation that the Trump administration will bring major shifts to the global economy and to its approach to alliances.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, The Hill

item.title: Donald Trump's incoming team met with Ukraine officials
item.description: Trump's team is trying to bring Ukraine and Russia to the negotiating table quickly, potentially through diplomatic coercion.
item.dc:creator: Safia Samee Ali, NewsNation

item.title: Qatar returns to Hamas-Israel negotiations as Trump envoy looks to make inroads
item.description: Qatar has once again resumed its top role in helping negotiate a cease-fire between Israel and Hamas to return hostages in Gaza, as President-elect Donald Trump's Middle East envoy begins meeting with top leaders amid crisis.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: 12-year-old girl finds ancient Egyptian amulet in Israel
item.description: An ordinary family trip turned extraordinary when a 12-year-old girl found what turned out to be an ancient Egyptian amulet dating back 3,500 years.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: South Korean president to face impeachment vote this weekend over martial law order, lawmakers say
item.description: South Korean opposition lawmakers will hold a vote to impeach President Yoon Suk Yeol after the president declared martial law on Tuesday, citing "anti-state" activities by his opponents.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Israeli strikes on a Gaza tent camp kill at least 21 people, hospital says
item.description: KHAN YOUNIS, Gaza Strip (AP) — Israeli airstrikes tore through a tent camp for displaced Palestinians in southern Gaza on Wednesday, sparking fires and killing at least 21 people, according to the head of a nearby hospital, in the latest assault on a sprawling tent city that Israel designated a huma
item.dc:creator: MOHAMMAD JAHJOUH and WAFAA SHURAFA, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: US 'seriously concerned' by developments in South Korea
item.description: The United States was surprised by South Korean President Yoon Suk Yeol’s brief declaration of emergency martial law.
item.dc:creator: Tanya Noury, NewsNation

item.title: Supreme Court wrestles with Hungary’s effort to end Holocaust survivor lawsuit
item.description: The Supreme Court on Tuesday probed the international consequences of allowing a group of Holocaust survivors and their heirs to leverage U.S. courts to seek compensation from Hungary for confiscating their property.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, NewsNation

item.title: Elton John says he has lost his eyesight, struggles to see new musical
item.description: Elton John says he struggled to watch his new musical because he has lost his eyesight after contracting an infection.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation
